description: Fermi estimates expressed as words, triples, formulas, sources and values, following the same schema as the meteorology base data. Contains the disinformation-dam expected-value estimate discussed in the categorical-imperative working paper, and a meteorology-native worked example (raindrops in a thunderstorm).

words.name: fermi estimate
words.description: order-of-magnitude estimation that decomposes an unknown quantity into a product of roughly knowable factors

words.name: disinformation dam expected value
words.description: expected avoided harm of building a regulatory dam against deliberate flooding of public discourse with disinformation, used to decide whether testing the measure is worthwhile

words.name: potential harm from democratic decline
words.description: monetised order-of-magnitude harm if the digital-overstimulation-to-democratic-decline link is real, dominated by populism-induced economic loss across affected economies

words.name: probability of the causal link
words.description: subjective probability that digital sensory overload at least partly causes the observed cognitive and democratic decline

words.name: leverage of the measure
words.description: fraction of the potential harm that a feasible, rule-of-law-bound regulatory dam could plausibly avert

words.name: cost of the test
words.description: order-of-magnitude cost and risk of running the regulatory experiment, including risk to legitimate free expression

words.name: expected value to cost ratio
words.description: ratio of expected avoided harm to test cost; a value far above one indicates the test is worthwhile

words.name: raindrops in a thunderstorm
words.description: order-of-magnitude number of raindrops falling from a single mid-size thunderstorm cell over its lifetime

words.name: storm cell area
words.description: ground area covered by a single mid-size thunderstorm cell

words.name: total rainfall depth
words.description: depth of water deposited by the storm over its lifetime

words.name: total rain volume
words.description: volume of water deposited by the storm, the area times the rainfall depth

words.name: raindrop volume
words.description: volume of a single representative raindrop

words.name: decision under uncertainty
words.description: choosing an action when outcomes and their probabilities are only roughly known

formulas.name: disinformation dam expected value
formulas.expression: "disinformation dam expected value" = "potential harm from democratic decline" * "probability of the causal link" * "leverage of the measure"

formulas.name: expected value to cost ratio
formulas.expression: "expected value to cost ratio" = "disinformation dam expected value" / "cost of the test"

formulas.name: total rain volume
formulas.expression: "total rain volume" = "storm cell area" * "total rainfall depth"

formulas.name: raindrops in a thunderstorm
formulas.expression: "raindrops in a thunderstorm" = "total rain volume" / "raindrop volume"

sources.name: fermi estimate
sources.description: an order-of-magnitude figure assumed for a Fermi estimation, deliberately rough and chosen to be defensible within a factor of a few

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured
